author: gb9t
content:
Lints referenced from 00-rust-conventions.md REQ-11 / forbidden-patterns table:

- unsafe_without_proof — rejects 'unsafe { ... }' lacking '// SAFETY:' AND '// PROOF:' annotations in preceding 8 lines
- panic_in_kernel_path — rejects panic!, unwrap, expect, unreachable!, todo!, unimplemented! in production code paths
- async_in_kernel — rejects async fn, async {}, .await, Future, executor types
- bare_arithmetic_on_size — rejects bare +, -, *, / on usize/u64/u32 size types (require checked_*/saturating_*/wrapping_*)
- alloc_without_gfp — rejects Box::new, Vec::new, Vec::push (require KBox/KVec with explicit GFP flag)
- unstable_feature_without_decision — rejects new #![feature(...)] not in upstream rust-for-linux's allowed set

These are project-specific clippy plugins. They MUST land before any non-foundation Rust implementation work because the entire codebase needs to grow under their enforcement, not be retrofitted later.

Recommendation in 00-rust-conventions.md Q1: block subsystem-implementation work on these landing first.

Block by issue #1 (Phase A foundation work).
